summary: Adventure chunk from Into the Outdoors with Gun and Camera page 130, chunk 3.

source_text.source_title: Into the Outdoors with Gun and Camera

mechanics.raw_text: For years no power has been available for any of these electronic enter- tainments, but the tribe maintains a battered emnant of its gear and continues to collect films, tapes, and records whenever they come across shem, Black Disk tribesmen dess in leather and fur getups popular in Hollywood caveman ‘movies. In addition to the phono records around the neck, they wear other odd bits of ‘metal, plastic, and glass from the Golden Age = kitchen utensil, tin cans, transistors, printed circuits, door knobs, toilet fixtures, etc. — presenting quite a colorful appearance to an observer, ‘The savages speak in Hollywood Indian (Me heap hungry. Me gottum wampum, You ‘gottum grub?” liberally salted with slang from the 1960's, 70's, and '80's (“real cool cat,” hot dog,” “groovy,” "cosmic," “totaly rad,” “to the max,” "tubular, man”), Linguistcaly, such a dialect is pretty implausible, but for tone and atmosphere i's perfect, showing that the tribesmen have reverted to savagery (the Hollywood Indian bit), though they've retain- ‘ed odd and disarticulated fragments of the disappeared civilized culture (the period slang) Tes also possible to improvise this garbled pidgin English pretty easily, permitting you to stay in character during dialogues, (By con- trast, making up a plausible future dialect like ‘Burgess does in Clockwork Orange, is ‘way too much work and impossible to improvise without lots of study.) Here's a sample dialogue: Greg: | jar a knife up next to the Commie’s throat and order him to reveal the names of his fellow-travelers, GM: Me no know what_mean, ‘fellow- travelers’. You bozos heap cuckoo, Me Black Disk clan. What clan you groovy cats from?
